proposed_domain: Persian qanat (underground horizontal aqueduct: mother well + sloping infiltration gallery + spaced ventilation shafts + gravity-fed water emergence at distant mazhar)

closest_prior_match.domain: Roman aqueduct (above-ground stone arches with constant slope)
closest_prior_match.verdict: CLOSE — Roman aqueduct is surface-level constant-slope channel; qanat is subsurface infiltration-with-shafts. Different mechanism class (gravity-conveyance vs gravity-with-water-table-tapping-via-shafts).

decision: ACCEPT-WITH-CAVEAT — Roman aqueduct overlap noted but mechanism class differs (qanat actively taps phreatic surface via infiltration gallery + multi-shaft ventilation; Roman aqueduct merely conveys).
